pattern: Greedily grow the current partition digit by digit; the moment appending the next digit would push the running value above k, close the partition there and start fresh -- any digit alone exceeding k makes the whole string unpartitionable.
hints: Build up the current partition's numeric value one digit at a time, the same way you'd read the number left to right.
The moment adding the next digit would push the running value past k, that's your cue to end the current partition right before this digit and begin a new one with it.
If any single digit by itself already exceeds k, no partition can ever include it, so the whole string is impossible -- return -1 immediately.

edge_inputs.description: the whole string already fits under a very large k
k is small enough that every digit must stand alone
two moderate digits individually fit but together exceed k
a partition ends right at the boundary before the next digit would push it over
digits of varying size force partitions of different lengths
a digit exactly equal to k is still allowed on its own

stress.description: random digit strings with k sometimes tiny (forcing many partitions or impossibility) and sometimes huge
